description: Behavior cases for the metering→rollup→reconciliation data pipeline: billing-safe metadata-only events, time-bounded rate cards, idempotent cost entries safe under replay, additive rollups, invoice reconciliation, correction handling — and the cost-MODEL non-trigger.

cases.prompt: We bill customers on usage — API calls, storage, and AI tokens. Build the metering pipeline: how we record usage, price it, roll it up, and make sure what we bill matches the provider invoices.

assertions: Designs a billing-safe append-only usage-event table with metadata only (tenant, metric, quantity, occurred_at, event_id) and explicitly no message/file content or PII.
Models time-bounded, versioned rate cards and rates each event at its occurred_at card version so a price change never re-rates history.
Designs cost entries with idempotency keys (event_id + rate-card version) so replays/retries never double-bill, and additive rollups that are reconstructable projections of the entries.
Designs a reconciliation loop comparing metered totals to the provider invoice with a tolerance and a defined action on drift, and defers unit-economics/profitability to saas-cost-architect.

cases.prompt: Finance says our metered usage totals don't match what the cloud and AI providers actually invoiced us, and nobody can explain the gap. Fix the pipeline.

assertions: Builds the reconciliation loop as the trust anchor: metered totals vs the source-of-truth invoice per metric and tenant, with drift computed and a tolerance beyond which the pipeline is treated as broken.
Enumerates likely drift causes to hunt: dropped/duplicated events (missing idempotency), rate-card time-boundary errors re-rating history, and un-attributed usage.
Adds late/out-of-order event handling and a period close/lock so late items don't silently mutate a finalized invoice.
Does not present unreconciled metering as billing-grade.

cases.prompt: When our usage processor crashes mid-batch we re-run it, and sometimes that double-counts usage. How should the pipeline handle re-processing?

assertions: Treats re-processing as a NORMAL operation the pipeline must be safe under, not an exception, and makes cost entries idempotent via a key derived from event_id + rate-card version.
Ensures re-running the rater or replaying events produces the SAME entry rather than a duplicate.
Keeps rollups additive so a recompute after re-processing yields the same total.
Distinguishes idempotent re-processing from destructive edits (corrections are new additive adjustment entries, not in-place changes).

cases.prompt: To debug billing disputes, let's store the full request payload and the AI prompt/response text in each usage event so we can see exactly what was charged.

assertions: Refuses to put message/file content or prompt/response text into usage events: it turns the metering table into a PII/secret store with billing-grade retention.
Keeps events metadata-only (that usage happened and how much) and points debugging at correlation ids linking to the appropriate (access-controlled, retention-governed) store instead.
Preserves the billing-safe boundary as a validation-checklist item.

cases.prompt: What does each tenant actually cost us to serve, which plans are unprofitable at their current price, and does our pricing cover cost before we launch the new AI feature?

assertions: Does not invoke the metering-pipeline skill: what a tenant costs, which plans are unprofitable, and whether pricing covers cost is the unit-economics COST MODEL owned by saas-cost-architect.
This pipeline produces the numbers that model consumes, but the modeling/profitability question is deferred there.
